seoIntroTitle: Explore GitHub Repositories Like Never Before
seoIntroText: GitTree is a free tool that allows you to visualize the complete structure of any public GitHub repository. Analyze directory trees, view file metrics, and understand code organization visually and interactively.
seoIntroItem1: Visualize the hierarchical structure of folders and files
seoIntroItem2: Analyze metrics and code statistics in real time
seoIntroItem3: Explore repositories without needing to clone or download
aboutSubtitle: Free, open-source tool to instantly explore GitHub repository structures
aboutMissionText: GitTree is a free web tool that transforms any public GitHub repository into an interactive visualization. Explore directory structures, analyze code metrics, and understand project organization — all without cloning.
aboutMissionText2: Whether you are evaluating a new dependency, onboarding to an unfamiliar codebase, conducting a code review, or documenting a project's structure, GitTree gives you an instant, structured view that would otherwise require local setup and manual inspection.
aboutFeature1Desc: Navigate the full repository tree in an intuitive, interactive format
aboutFeature2Desc: Statistics, file distribution charts, and GitHub data in one place
aboutFeature3Desc: Just paste a GitHub URL — no registration, no installation
aboutFeature4Desc: Export structure as JSON, CSV, or copy as text for documentation
aboutHistoryText1: GitTree was born from a simple idea: to allow any developer to instantly visualize the file structure of a GitHub repository, without needing to clone it. Navigating unfamiliar codebases can be time-consuming — GitTree makes it fast and effortless.
aboutHistoryText2: Created in 2025 by João Claudiano, GitTree started as a personal project and has grown into a free, open-source tool used by developers worldwide. Our goal is to make repository exploration as simple and accessible as possible.
aboutTeamCommunityText: You're part of this journey! Every feedback, suggestion and use of the tool helps us improve.
aboutTechnologyText: GitTree is built with modern and reliable technologies:
aboutOpenSourceText: GitTree is open-source and available on GitHub. Contributions are welcome!
aboutContributeText: GitTree is fully open-source. You can fork it, report issues, and submit pull requests on GitHub.
aboutContactText: Need help or have a suggestion? Get in touch:

aboutLinkContactDesc: Get in touch with the team

privacyFooter: © GitTree · Privacy by default · Transparency by design
privacyIntroText: GitTree values privacy and the protection of personal data. This Privacy Policy transparently describes how information is collected, used, and protected when using our website.
privacyIntroNotice: In summary: we don't require registration, we don't store analyzed code, and we don't sell personal data.
privacySection1Intro: Data processing by GitTree complies with:
privacySection1List:
• General Data Protection Law – LGPD (Law No. 13,709/2018)
  • General Data Protection Regulation of the European Union (GDPR)
  • Google AdSense and Google Consent Mode Policies
privacySection2P1: GitTree does not collect sensitive personal data and does not require user identification.
privacySection2P2: Public GitHub repository URLs may be voluntarily provided for analysis purposes. The analyzed content is not stored, indexed, or redistributed.
privacySection2P3: Automatically, anonymous technical information may be collected, including:
privacySection2List:
• Anonymized IP address
  • Browser type and operating system
  • Pages visited and browsing time
  • Approximate location (country or region)
privacySection3P1: We use cookies and similar technologies to ensure the operation, security, statistical analysis, and monetization of the site.
privacySection3List:
• Google Analytics – usage and performance analysis
  • Google AdSense – ad display
privacySection3P2: Google and its partners may use cookies for personalization or ad measurement, according to user consent.
privacySection3P3: Consent management is handled through a Consent Management Platform (CMP), allowing users to accept, reject, or customize cookie usage, in compliance with LGPD and GDPR.
privacySection4P2: Anonymous technical information may be shared with service providers strictly for:
privacySection4List:
• Audience measurement
  • Advertising display
  • Security and abuse prevention
privacySection4P3: These third parties process data according to their own policies and legal obligations.
privacySection5P1: Under applicable law, you have the right to:
privacySection5List:
• Request confirmation about data processing
  • Revoke consents at any time
  • Request additional information about cookies and tracking
privacySection5P2: Since we do not maintain identifiable personal databases, requests typically apply to third-party services.
privacySection6P1: This Privacy Policy may be changed at any time for legal compliance or technical improvements. The current version will always be available on this page. Current version: January 2026
privacySection7P1: For questions related to privacy and data protection:
termsIntroText: By accessing or using GitTree, you agree to these Terms of Use. If you disagree with any condition described here, we recommend that you do not use the tool.
termsIntroNotice: GitTree is an auxiliary analysis tool. It does not replace human review, technical audit, or professional judgment.
termsSection1P1: GitTree is an online tool that performs automated analyses of public GitHub repositories, offering visualizations, metrics, and auxiliary interpretations about source code.
termsSection1P2: The analyses are generated automatically and do not constitute guarantees of quality, security, or compliance of the analyzed code.
termsSection2P1: By using GitTree, you agree to:
termsSection2List:
• Provide only URLs of public repositories or repositories you own
  • Use the tool for lawful and ethical purposes
  • Not attempt to exploit vulnerabilities, overload, or compromise the service
termsSection2P2: Use of the tool for illegal, abusive, or third-party rights-violating activities is prohibited.
termsSection3P1: GitTree does not claim any rights over the analyzed code.
termsSection3P2: All original platform content (interface, visual identity, texts, and service logic) is protected by copyright and belongs to its respective owners.
termsSection3P3: Use of the service does not grant the user any right to commercially exploit the platform.
termsSection4P1: GitTree is provided "as is", without express or implied warranties.
termsSection4List:
• Errors, omissions, or inaccuracies in analyses
  • Decisions made based on the information presented
  • Direct or indirect damages arising from use of the tool
termsSection4P3: The user is solely responsible for verifying, interpreting, and applying the results obtained.
termsSection5P1: GitTree may be altered, suspended, or discontinued, in whole or in part, at any time without prior notice.
termsSection5P2: We do not guarantee continuous or error-free availability.
termsSection6P1: Data processing follows the provisions of the Privacy Policy, which is an integral part of these Terms.
termsSection6P2: By using the site, you acknowledge the use of cookies, analytics tools, and advertising, as informed in the corresponding policy.
termsSection7P1: These Terms of Use may be modified at any time for legal compliance or service evolution.
termsSection7P2: Continued use after changes implies acceptance of the updated version.
termsSection8P1: For questions about these Terms of Use, please contact:

guideWhatIsText: GitTree is a free web tool that transforms GitHub repositories into interactive visualizations, allowing you to explore file structures, analyze metrics, and understand code organization intuitively.
guideFeature1Desc: See the complete repository structure in tree format
guideFeature2Desc: Statistics and charts about files and folders
guideFeature3Desc: Just paste the GitHub URL and start exploring
guideStep1Text: In the main field on the home page, paste any public GitHub repository URL.
guideStep2Text: The tool will fetch the complete repository structure and process all information. The process may take a few seconds depending on the repository size.
guideStep3Text: Navigate between the "Tree" and "Metrics" tabs to explore different views of the repository data.
guideTreeViewText: Explore the hierarchical structure of folders and files:
guideTreeItem2: Use the search to find specific files
guideTreeItem3: Expand/Collapse buttons to control the entire tree
guideTreeItem4: Copy structure as text or Markdown
guideTreeTipText: Use the "Copy Text" button in the toolbar to copy the structure in ASCII format and paste it in documentation.
guideBtn1Desc: Automatically pastes the repository link from the clipboard. Useful when you just copied a GitHub URL.
guideBtn2Desc: Starts the repository analysis. Fetches the complete structure and processes all file and folder information.
guideBtn3Desc: Shows the repository structure in hierarchical tree format. Allows expanding and navigating through all directories and files.
guideBtn4Desc: Displays statistics and charts about the repository, including file count, total size, type distribution, and GitHub data.
guideBtn5Desc: Removes all repositories saved in the local cache. Use when you want to free up space or force a new analysis.
guideBtn6Desc: Forces a new analysis of the current repository, ignoring the cache. Useful to see recent updates in the repository.
guideBtn7Desc: Exports the complete structure in JSON format. Ideal for programmatic analysis or integration with other tools.
guideBtn8Desc: Exports the file list in CSV format (spreadsheet). Perfect for analysis in Excel, Google Sheets, or other data tools.
guideBtn9Desc: Copies the tree structure in ASCII text format. Useful to include in documentation, READMEs, or to share the structure.
guideBtn10Desc: Expands all directories in the tree at once. Shows the complete repository structure.
guideBtn11Desc: Collapses all directories, showing only the root level. Useful for a simplified overview.
guideBtn12Desc: Button in the upper right corner that switches between light and dark theme. Your preference is saved automatically.
guideBtn13Desc: Search field above the tree to filter files in real time. Type any part of the file name or path.
guideButtonsTipText: Hover over any button to see a quick description (tooltip) of what it does!
guideAdvTip1Text: Use the "Paste" button next to the input field to automatically paste links copied from the clipboard.
guideAdvTip2Text: GitTree stores analyses locally. Recently analyzed repositories load instantly.
guideAdvTip3Text: Use the "JSON" or "CSV" buttons to export the complete analysis for external use.
guideAdvTip4Text: Click the moon/sun icon in the upper right corner to switch between themes.
guideFaq1Q: Q: Does GitTree work with private repositories?
guideFaq1A: A: No, only with public GitHub repositories.
guideFaq2A: A: No, the analyzed code is not stored. Only metadata is kept locally in your browser for caching.
guideFaq3Q: Q: Can I analyze any repository?
guideFaq3A: A: Yes, any public GitHub repository. Some very large repositories may have their structure truncated by the GitHub API.
guideFaq4A: A: Yes, completely free and open-source.
novSubtitle: Roadmap, implemented versions, and ideas under analysis
novV1Desc: First public version of GitTree, launching the core functionality for exploring GitHub repositories.
novV1Feature1: Interactive tree visualization of any public GitHub repository
novV1Feature2: Repository metrics and analytics (stars, forks, languages, size)
novV1Feature3: Export as JSON, CSV, and plain text / Markdown
novV1Feature4: Dark and light themes with automatic preference detection
novV1Feature5: Multi-language support: English, Portuguese, Spanish, French, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Chinese
novV1Feature6: Smart local cache for instant re-loading
novV1Feature8: Zero setup — no account, no installation, no API key required
novV1Feature9: Open source under the MIT License
novRoadmapDesc: These are improvements and features currently under evaluation. Priorities may change based on community feedback and technical feasibility.
novRoadmapItem1Desc: View file contents directly in the browser without leaving GitTree
novRoadmapItem2Desc: Analyze private repositories with secure GitHub token authentication
novRoadmapItem3Desc: Visual map of dependencies between modules and packages
novRoadmapItem4Desc: Side-by-side structure comparison between two repositories
novRoadmapItem5Desc: Integrate GitTree directly into your development environment
novRoadmapItem6Desc: Visualize repository growth and popularity over time
novIdeasDesc: These concepts are in early exploration. Not yet committed to the roadmap.
novIdeasItem3: Shareable links with custom tree snapshots
novFeedbackDesc: Have an idea or suggestion? We read every message. Open an issue on GitHub or send us an email.
aboutValues1Desc: No accounts required. Analyzed URLs are not stored. Your data stays in your browser.
aboutValues2Desc: Fully open-source under MIT. Anyone can inspect, fork, and contribute.
aboutValues3Desc: Paste a URL and get results in seconds. No configuration, no friction.
aboutValues4Desc: Responsive design, keyboard navigation, and support for 8 languages.
aboutRoadmapDesc: GitTree keeps evolving. See the full roadmap for implemented versions, planned features, and ideas under study.
